pi_items.source: Verified against export_result.sh line 19 and setup_task.sh directory creation logic

Contradiction found: metadata claims 'min_unique_values': 10 (implying >=10), but verifier.py line 72 checks 'if not is_binary and unique_vals > 10' (requiring strictly >10, not >=10). An output with exactly 10 unique values would meet metadata requirement but fail verifier.
Verified against verifier.py line 58 which checks for 'percent_zeros > 20.0', confirming background should be 0 (black pixels). Consistent with task description.

Contradiction found: metadata claims [255] is forbidden, but verifier.py does not explicitly forbid 255. Instead, verifier penalizes high mean intensity values. An output with mean_nonzero=255 gets 15 pts instead of 30 pts (line 80-81), not a complete failure. 255 is discouraged but not explicitly forbidden.

Downloaded and analyzed from https://imagej.net/ij/images/blobs.gif. Specifications: 256x254 pixels, GIF format with inverted palette, 31 unique grayscale values (7-247 after display conversion), mean=151.73, dark objects (mean~99.53) comprise ~52.8% of pixels, bright background (mean~210.24) comprises ~47.2%. Objects labeled as 'particles are dark (~40-60)' in export_result.sh actually have mean closer to 99.53 (in the 40-100 range per verifier.py).

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official ImageJ Blobs (25K) sample image (256x254 pixels, GIF format, 31 unique grayscale values ranging 7-247). The expected output should be saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/masked_blobs.tif as an 8-bit TIFF. The output must have non-zero pixels (representing preserved object intensities) with a background of zero. The task requires the output to have more than 10 unique grayscale values (not exactly 10) and expects non-zero pixels to have mean intensity less than 150 (typical object values ~40-100 range). The image contains dark blob objects (mean ~99.53) against a bright background (mean ~210.24).
